  "text": "The Kafka log retention policy is enforced across several Java/Scala source files in the apache/kafka repository:\n\n1. **Config Definitions** (`clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/TopicConfig.java`): Defines the canonical config keys `retention.ms` (RETENTION_MS_CONFIG), `retention.bytes` (RETENTION_BYTES_CONFIG), `local.retention.ms` (LOCAL_LOG_RETENTION_MS_CONFIG), and `local.retention.bytes` (LOCAL_LOG_RETENTION_BYTES_CONFIG).\n\n2. **Server-Level Config** (`server-common/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/ServerLogConfigs.java`): Defines broker-level synonyms `log.retention.ms` (LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG) and `log.retention.bytes` (LOG_RETENTION_BYTES_CONFIG), with defaults and documentation.\n\n3. **Config Synonyms** (`server-common/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.java`): Maps broker config names to topic config names, including retention.ms → retention.minutes → retention.hours cascade.\n\n4. **LogConfig Validation** (`storage/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/storage/internals/log/LogConfig.java`): Central log configuration class that defines retention settings, validates local vs. total retention constraints (`validateRetentionConfigsWhenRemoteCopyDisabled`, `validateRemoteStorageRetentionSize`, `validateRemoteStorageRetentionTime`, `validateTopicLogConfigValues`), and exposes `localRetentionMs()` and `localRetentionBytes()` accessors.\n\n5. **Segment Deletion Enforcement** (`storage/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/storage/internals/log/UnifiedLog.java`): Implements the actual segment deletion logic:\n   - `deleteOldSegments()`: Main entry point, delegates to time-based and size-based checks\n   - `deleteRetentionMsBreachedSegments()`: Time-based retention — deletes segments whose largest timestamp is older than retentionMs\n   - `deleteRetentionSizeBreachedSegments()`: Size-based retention — deletes oldest segments until total log size ≤ retentionSize\n   - `deleteLogStartOffsetBreachedSegments()`: Deletes segments before the log start offset\n\n6. **LogManager Retention Check** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/log/LogManager.scala`): The `cleanupLogs()` method is scheduled periodically (controlled by `retentionCheckMs`) and iterates over all non-compacted partitions calling `log.deleteOldSegments()` on each.\n\n7. **KafkaConfig Validation** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala`): The `validateValues()` method enforces that `log.retention.ms` is either -1 (unlimited) or ≥ 1. Also defines `logRetentionTimeMillis` and `logRetentionBytes` accessors that resolve the ms/minutes/hours config cascade.\n\n8. **LogManagerBuilder** (`core/src/main/java/kafka/server/builders/LogManagerBuilder.java`): Builder that wires `retentionCheckMs` into the LogManager construction."

  "text": "The Kafka log retention policy is enforced across several Java/Scala source files in the apache/kafka repository:\n\n1. **Config Definitions** (`clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/TopicConfig.java`): Defines the canonical config keys `retention.ms` (RETENTION_MS_CONFIG), `retention.bytes` (RETENTION_BYTES_CONFIG), `local.retention.ms` (LOCAL_LOG_RETENTION_MS_CONFIG), and `local.retention.bytes` (LOCAL_LOG_RETENTION_BYTES_CONFIG).\n\n2. **Server-Level Config** (`server-common/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/ServerLogConfigs.java`): Defines broker-level synonyms `log.retention.ms` (LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG) and `log.retention.bytes` (LOG_RETENTION_BYTES_CONFIG), with defaults and documentation.\n\n3. **Config Synonyms** (`server-common/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.java`): Maps broker config names to topic config names, including retention.ms → retention.minutes → retention.hours cascade.\n\n4. **LogConfig Validation** (`storage/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/storage/internals/log/LogConfig.java`): Central log configuration class that defines retention settings, validates local vs. total retention constraints (`validateRetentionConfigsWhenRemoteCopyDisabled`, `validateRemoteStorageRetentionSize`, `validateRemoteStorageRetentionTime`, `validateTopicLogConfigValues`), and exposes `localRetentionMs()` and `localRetentionBytes()` accessors.\n\n5. **Segment Deletion Enforcement** (`storage/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/storage/internals/log/UnifiedLog.java`): Implements the actual segment deletion logic:\n   - `deleteOldSegments()`: Main entry point, delegates to time-based and size-based checks\n   - `deleteRetentionMsBreachedSegments()`: Time-based retention — deletes segments whose largest timestamp is older than retentionMs\n   - `deleteRetentionSizeBreachedSegments()`: Size-based retention — deletes oldest segments until total log size ≤ retentionSize\n   - `deleteLogStartOffsetBreachedSegments()`: Deletes segments before the log start offset\n\n6. **LogManager Retention Check** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/log/LogManager.scala`): The `cleanupLogs()` method is scheduled periodically (controlled by `retentionCheckMs`) and iterates over all non-compacted partitions calling `log.deleteOldSegments()` on each.\n\n7. **KafkaConfig Validation** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala`): The `validateValues()` method enforces that `log.retention.ms` is either -1 (unlimited) or ≥ 1. Also defines `logRetentionTimeMillis` and `logRetentionBytes` accessors that resolve the ms/minutes/hours config cascade.\n\n8. **LogManagerBuilder** (`core/src/main/java/kafka/server/builders/LogManagerBuilder.java`): Builder that wires `retentionCheckMs` into the LogManager construction."
